text: The surgeon clips lumbar veins to manage the inferior vena cava safely. After dissecting the inferior vena cava and right renal vein, two vessel loops are placed. The three-D virtual model is overlapped onto the real anatomy using TilePro technology from the DaVinci Surgical Console, with a dedicated assistant performing hand-assisted overlapping using a three-D mouse. Augmented reality technology allows the surgeon to hide elements of the virtual model, enhancing the perception of the thrombus extension into the inferior vena cava.

The surgeon fine-tunes the vessel loops using virtual model guidance, interrupts the caval blood flow by tightening the loops and securing them with hemo-locks. A cavotomy is performed on the anterior face of the ostium with the renal vein, revealing the tumor thrombus beneath the venous wall. The thrombus is gently removed from the vena cava, and the caval wall is sutured with a continuous 4-0 suture secured with a lapra tie. The vessel loops are removed, restoring venous return, and the radical nephrectomy is completed.
